This feeling is a wave. Waves pass.
You are not back there. You are here, and here is safe.
You don't have to fix anything right now.
Your body is trying to protect you. It's okay, you can tell it to rest.
This is uncomfortable, not dangerous.
You have survived every panic that came before this one.
Right now, in this exact moment, you are okay.
Nothing is required of you for the next few minutes.
Your heart is beating fast because it's trying to help, not because something is wrong.
You are allowed to just sit here and breathe.
This will pass, the way it always has.
You are safe enough to slow down.
It's okay if your thoughts are loud right now. You don't have to answer them.
You are not alone in this moment.
Your feet are on the ground. The ground is steady.
You don't have to understand this feeling to get through it.
You are held, even if it doesn't feel that way yet.
This moment is temporary, even when it doesn't feel like it.
You can be scared and still be safe at the same time.
Nothing bad is happening right now, in this exact second.
You are allowed to take up space with this feeling.
Your only job right now is to breathe.
You've done this before. You know how to come back from this.
It's okay to need a minute. Take as many as you need.
You are not too much for this moment.
This room is safe. This chair is safe. This breath is safe.
Whatever this is, it doesn't get the final word.
You are still you, even in the middle of this.
Let your shoulders drop, just a little.
You are closer to calm than it feels like right now.
